data.title: A.I.S. Resources announces private placement financing, debt transaction and CEO transition
data.description:
• A.I.S. Resources (AIS:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 2.86M units at a price of $0.035/unit for gross proceeds of $100,000.
  • The proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • Each private placement unit consists of one common share and one transferable share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one additional common share for a period of 2 years from the closing date of the offering at $0.05/share.
  • AIS has also entered into debt settlement agreements with various creditors to settle an aggregate amount of $625,444 in outstanding debt related to services provided to AIS through the issuance of an aggregate of 12.51M shares at $0.05/share.
  • AIS announced the appointment of Marc Enright-Morin as president and chief executive of the company.
  • Andrew Neale resigned as CEO and director.
  • Morin has served as CEO, president, and director of multiple publicly traded companies in the resource sector.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: OSI Systems receives $32M security order
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) announced that its security division received an international order valued at ~$32M to provide radio frequency based critical systems that are designed for long-range secure communications.
  • The company is expected to support the systems integration and deployment activities for this program.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: QCR announces CEO retirement, CFO Todd Gipple appointed as successor
data.description:
• Multi-bank holding company QCR Holdings (NASDAQ:QCRH) announced on Monday the retirement of Larry Helling from the CEO post.
  • Helling is also set to retire from the board.
  • President and CFO Todd Gipple has been appointed the president and CEO.
  • Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer Nick Anderson is set to become the CFO.
  • The retirement and the consequent appointments are effective immediately following the May 22 stockholders meeting.
  • QCRH shares were trading -0.48% pre-market at $76.21.

data.title: Tesla prepares to deploy full self driving features in China
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is preparing a software update for its customers in China, offering driver-assistance features similar to the full self-driving capabilities available in the US, according to a Bloomberg report.

The update will enable Tesla owners to access driver-assist features on city streets, such as guiding vehicles to exit ramps and intersections, recognizing traffic signals, and managing lane changes and speed adjustments.

As per the report, these capabilities will be available to customers who have paid 64,000 yuan for FSD. The feature will initially roll out on select models in the coming days, with plans for gradual expansion.

Chief executive Elon Musk has been seeking regulatory approval for FSD to operate on Chinese roads for a few years. The company has been facing challenges with FSD in China due to restrictions from both Beijing and Washington on how the system is trained to navigate local roads.

To overcome this, Tesla engineers were using publicly available videos of Chinese streets from the internet to train the system. Musk aims to launch the FSD system in China this year.

TSLA shares were up 1.8% premarket on Monday.

data.title: CarGurus CFO Elisa Palazzo to step down
data.description:
• CarGurus (NASDAQ:CARG) announced Monday that effective March 7, Elisa Palazzo will step down as the company’s chief financial officer.
  • Ms. Palazzo was appointed the company's CFO in October 2023.
  • CEO Jason Trevisan will serve as the company’s principal financial and accounting officer beginning March 7 until Ms. Palazzo’s successor is named.
  • Shares opened 2% lower, below the 200-day moving average for the first time since August.

data.title: Smithfield Foods gains after seeing mostly bull ratings after its post-IPO quiet period expires
data.description:
Smithfield Foods (NASDAQ:SFD) opened on Monday with a swing higher after the post-IPO quiet period on the stock expired on Monday to free up analysts to post ratings.

The food processing stock landed a majority of bull ratings, including from Morgan Stanley (Overweight, price target $28), Barclays (Overweight, PT $28), Citi (Buy, PT $27), UBS (Buy, PT $26), Bank of America (Buy, PT $28), and Goldman Sachs (Buy, PT $32).

BofA's Buy rating on Smithfield Foods (NASDAQ:SFD) was based on its view that the company has a superior financial profile versus its closest peers, supported by its solid execution and favorablemacroeconomic trends. "SFD has been able to expand margins, gain share, and innovate in a difficult category," highlighted analyst Peter Galbo. It was also highlighted that high-protein diets are on trend for U.S. consumers and that the pork cycle is currently advantaged versus beef and chicken.

Meanwhile, Exane BNP Paribas came in more cautious on Smithfield Foods (SFD), with a Neutral rating and price target of $23.

Shares of Smithfield Foods (SFD) were up 1.9% in early trading on Monday to $21.50 vs. the post-IPO range of $19.10 to $22.03. The IPO was priced at $20 per share.

data.title: Dow, S&P, Nasdaq advance as markets look to shake off Friday's sell-off
data.description:
Major averages climbed higher on Monday, as markets aim for a positive start to the week after U.S. stocks ended last week on a gloomy note.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.45%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.22%.

U.S. stocks tumbled Friday, as investors closed the week with gloomy economic updates and word of a government investigation into UnitedHealth (UNH) in hand.

"Now recapping last week, which ended with a big risk-off move on Friday following more stagflationary U.S. data alongside some scares around the reporting of the new coronavirus discovered by researchers at the Wuhan Institute of Virology," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

"However, the earlier data started the softness…the overall backdrop weighed on risk assets, with the S&P 500 posting its worst day of 2025 so far, leaving it -1.66% lower on the week," Reid added.

Investors will keep their eyes on some big earnings releases this week. Home Depot (HD) and Lowe's Companies (LOW) will report on Tuesday and Wednesday, respectively.

The performance of the two companies will be under the radar as investors look to gauge the demand from U.S. consumers.

Nvidia (NVDA) will release its results on Wednesday.

The Chicago Fed National Activity Index dove back into negative territory.

On the economic side, the February Dallas Fed Manufacturing Survey will be released shortly.

data.title: Siyata Mobile expands istribution in new agreement with IP Access International
data.description:
• Siyata Mobile (NASDAQ:SYTA) has announced a new distribution agreement with IP Access International, a provider of reliable communication solutions for organizations operating in challenging environments.
  • Through this partnership, IP Access International will offer Siyata's SD7 rugged PoC devices and accessories to its extensive customer base, which includes fire departments, mining operations, oil and energy companies, utilities, and other industries that require robust, mission-critical communication solutions in remote areas.
  • Siyata's SD7 devices will integrate seamlessly with IP Access International's SuperGIG™ solution, delivering unparalleled connectivity by combining major cellular carriers, OneWeb and Starlink LEO constellations, and a GEO satellite backup into a single, resilient network.
  • By incorporating Siyata's SD7 devices into the SuperGIG™ ecosystem, end users will benefit from uninterrupted push-to-talk capabilities, ensuring communication remains clear and accessible even in areas where traditional networks fall short.
  • Marc Seelenfreund, CEO of Siyata Mobile, commented, "We are thrilled to partner with IP Access International, as their customers align perfectly with Siyata's target market."

data.title: Newmont again delays decision on Yanacocha sulfides project in Peru
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) said late Friday that it will again postpone a final investment decision for its Yanacocha sulfides project in Peru, saying the delay will allow it to prioritize more profitable projects.

Yanacocha is South America's largest gold mine, located in Peru's Cajamarca department, and it also produces copper and silver.

In 2023, Newmont (NYSE:NEM) said it would defer its planned $300M investment decision on the Yanacocha project for a minimum of two years.

Newmont (NEM) has said it believes Yanacocha has the potential to become a Tier 1 gold and copper project, which means the mine could produce more than 500K oz/year of gold equivalent; the mine already has a mine life exceeding 2040, exceeding the necessary 10 years required for it to be a Tier 1 project in a country with a B or higher credit rating.

data.title: Salesforce inks multi-billion dollar deal with Google Cloud
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) has entered into a deal with Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) to offer its customer relations management software, Agentforce artificial intelligence assistants and Data Cloud offerings through Google Cloud, the companies announced today.

Google and Salesforce already have many of the same clients, and this new deal will allow for more product integration between Google Workspace and Salesforce's customer relationship management and AI offerings. Salesforce already uses Amazon (AMZN) Web Services for much of its cloud computing.

"Our mutual customers have asked us to be able to work more seamlessly across Salesforce and Google Cloud, and this expanded partnership will help them accelerate their AI transformations with agentic AI, state-of-the-art AI models, data analytics, and more," said Thomas Kurian, CEO of Google Cloud.

The deal is expected to total $2.5B over the next seven years, according to a report by Bloomberg.

The new partnership follows last month's announcement by Google Cloud that ServiceNow (NOW) will also offer its customer relationship management, IT service management and security incident response solutions on Google Distributed Cloud.

Salesforce plans to reveal its fourth quarter fiscal 2025 financial results and outlook post-market on Wednesday. A consensus estimate expects adjusted earnings per share of $2.61 on revenue of $10.04B for the quarter.
